_source: A. flavus urate oxidase D2-symmetric homotetramer interface residues, derived from PDB 1R56 (Retailleau et al. 2004, Acta Cryst D60:453-462) and Colloc'h et al. 1997 (Nat Struct Biol 4:947-952). Residues identified as participating in subunit-subunit contacts (within 4.5 Å of a residue on a neighboring subunit) in the biological assembly. Two distinct interface classes: (a) tight dimer interface (A-B, large buried surface area, contains the active-site shared loop), (b) tetramer interface (A-C and A-D, smaller).
_protein: Aspergillus flavus uricase, Q00511, full 302-aa precursor (UniProt mature CHAIN is 2..302 = 301 aa after iMet removal; AlphaFold model and this analysis use 1-302 precursor numbering)
_numbering: 1-indexed, matching UniProt Q00511 mature sequence (after iMet removal)
_method_note: Interface residue lists below are approximate footprints from published structural analyses; exact residue lists vary 1-2 positions between PDB depositions. Where a range is given, all residues in the closed interval are interface-contacting.

tight_dimer_interface.description: A-B subunit interface; largest buried surface (~2200 A^2 per monomer); contains the active-site shared loop where residues from both subunits contribute to substrate binding pocket.

tetramer_interface.description: A-C / A-D contacts; smaller dimer-of-dimers interface; mostly stabilizing salt bridges and hydrogen bonds rather than hydrophobic packing.

key_interface_salt_bridges.description: Salt-bridge pairs reported at the tetramer/dimer interfaces; load-bearing for pH-dependent stability. Lower pH protonates Glu/Asp, breaking these bridges and shifting the dimer/tetramer equilibrium toward dissociation.
key_interface_salt_bridges._caveat: Salt-bridge participants are inferred from the published interface footprint and the sequence pKa pattern, not directly extracted from PDB coordinates in this analysis. Wet-lab structural validation (e.g., DSF pH-titration or AUC at pH 4.5 vs. 7.0) would tighten these assignments.
